<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// 数据模型定义
// ============================================

class TestObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// 函数实现
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Create observer relationship and test notification
 *
 * @param observerName Name for the observer node
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @return std::pair<bool, int> Pair of whether observer was notified and call count
 */
std::pair<bool, int> test_observer_notification(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    // Create observer and target nodes
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(observerName);
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestObserver>(targetName);
    
    // Get the singleton observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Register nodes with the graph
    graph.addNode(observer);
    graph.addNode(target);
    
    // Add observer relationship: observer watches target
    graph.addObserver(observer, target);
    
    // Notify target of change
    target->valueChanged();
    
    // Return notification status and call count
    return {observer->wasChanged(), observer->getCallCount()};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// 函数实现
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test thread manager registration and safety check
 *
 * @param enableSafety Whether to enable thread safety
 * @return bool Whether thread safety is enabled after operations
 */
bool test_thread_manager_registration(bool enableSafety) {
    // 1. Get thread manager instance
    reaction::ThreadManager &threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // 2. Register current thread
    threadManager.registerThread();
    
    // 3. Enable thread safety if requested
    if (enableSafety) {
        threadManager.enableThreadSafety();
    }
    
    // 4. Return thread safety status
    return threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

   - `reaction::React::get`: to get the current value of the reactive variable.

  2. Use `reaction::var` to create a reactive variable from `initialValue`.
  3. Use the `atomicAddAssign` method of the reactive variable to add `addValue` atomically.
  4. Use the `get` method to retrieve the current value and return it.

 Note: The function signature is given as:
        int test_atomic_operations(int initialValue, int addValue)

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// 函数实现
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test atomic operations on reactive variables
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value for the reactive variable
 * @param addValue Value to add atomically
 * @return int The final value after atomic addition
 */
int test_atomic_operations(int initialValue, int addValue) {
    // 创建响应式变量
    auto reactiveVar = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // 执行原子加法操作
    reactiveVar.atomicAddAssign(addValue);
    
    // 获取最终值并返回
    return reactiveVar.get();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

namespace test_reaction {

/**

 * @param a First input value
 * @param b Second input value
 * @return int The result of (a + b) * 2
 */
int test_reactive_calculation(int a, int b) {
    // 创建响应式变量
    auto var_a = reaction::var(a);
    auto var_b = reaction::var(b);
    
    // 创建响应式计算，依赖于 var_a 和 var_b
    auto calc_result = reaction::calc([](int x, int y) {
        return (x + y) * 2;
    }, var_a, var_b);
    
    // 返回计算结果
    return calc_result.get();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test observer node naming functionality
 *
 * @param name Name to set for the observer node
 * @return std::string The retrieved name from the observer graph
 */
std::string test_observer_naming(const std::string& name) {
    // 1. Create observer node
    auto node = std::make_unique<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    
    // 2. Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 3. Set node name
    graph.setName(node.get(), name);
    
    // 4. Return node name
    return graph.getName(node.get());
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test batch operations on observer nodes
 *
 * @return bool Whether batch operations work correctly
 */
bool test_batch_operations() {
    // 1. Create observer nodes
    auto node1 = reaction::ObserverNode::create();
    auto node2 = reaction::ObserverNode::create();
    
    // 2. Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 3. Create batch ID
    const void* batchId = graph.createBatchId();
    
    // 4. Register nodes to batch
    graph.addNodeToBatch(*node1, batchId);
    graph.addNodeToBatch(*node2, batchId);
    
    // 5. Verify nodes are in batch
    bool inBatch1 = node1->isInActiveBatch();
    bool inBatch2 = node2->isInActiveBatch();
    if (!inBatch1 || !inBatch2) {
        return false;
    }
    
    // 6. Unregister batch
    graph.unregisterActiveBatch(batchId);
    
    // 7. Verify nodes are no longer in batch
    bool outOfBatch1 = !node1->isInActiveBatch();
    bool outOfBatch2 = !node2->isInActiveBatch();
    if (!outOfBatch1 || !outOfBatch2) {
        return false;
    }
    
    // 8. Return success status
    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class TestObserverReset : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserverReset(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false), m_callCount(0) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test observer reset functionality
 *
 * @param observerName Name for the observer node
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @return bool Whether observer reset worked correctly (observer no longer notified after reset)
 */
bool test_observer_reset(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    // Create observer and target nodes
    std::unique_ptr<TestObserverReset> observer(new TestObserverReset(observerName));
    std::unique_ptr<TestObserverReset> target(new TestObserverReset(targetName));
    
    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Add observer relationship (target observes observer)
    target->addObserver(observer.get());
    
    // Notify target and verify observer was notified
    target->notify();
    if (observer->getCallCount() != 1) {
        return false; // Observer should have been notified once
    }
    
    // Record call count before reset
    int initialCallCount = observer->getCallCount();
    
    // Reset observer node
    observer->reset();
    
    // Notify target again
    target->notify();
    
    // Verify observer was not notified after reset
    if (observer->getCallCount() != initialCallCount) {
        return false; // Call count should remain unchanged after reset
    }
    
    return true; // Test passed
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

class TestObserverClosure : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserverClosure(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }

#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class TestTransactionalObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestTransactionalObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test transactional observer updates
 *
 * @param nodeName Name for the node to update
 * @return bool Whether transactional update succeeded (no cycle created)
 */
bool test_transactional_update(const std::string& nodeName) {
    // Create observer nodes
    auto source = std::make_shared<TestTransactionalObserver>("source");
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestTransactionalObserver>(nodeName);
    
    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Set up initial relationship: source observes target
    graph.addObserver(source, target);
    
    // Notify target and verify relationship works
    target->valueChanged(true);
    bool initialResponse = source->wasChanged();
    
    // Reset observer for next test
    source->reset();
    
    // Attempt transactional update that would create cycle
    bool transactionSuccess = false;
    try {
        // Try to create a cycle: source observes itself
        graph.updateObserversTransactional(source, {source});
        transactionSuccess = true; // Should not reach here if cycle exception is thrown
    } catch (const reaction::CycleException&) {
        transactionSuccess = false;
    }
    
    // Notify target again
    target->valueChanged(true);
    
    // Verify original relationship is preserved
    bool finalResponse = source->wasChanged();
    
    // Cleanup
    graph.closeNode(source);
    graph.closeNode(target);
    
    // Success if transaction failed (cycle exception thrown) and original relationship still works
    return !transactionSuccess && initialResponse && finalResponse;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

namespace test_reaction {

class TestMultipleObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestMultipleObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;

    int m_callCount = 0;

/**
 * Function: Test multiple observers watching a single target
 *
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @param observerNames Vector of names for observer nodes
 * @return std::pair<int, bool> Pair of total call count and whether all observers were notified
 */
std::pair<int, bool> test_multiple_observers(const std::string& targetName, const std::vector<std::string>& observerNames) {
    // Create target node
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(targetName);
    
    // Create observer nodes
    std::vector<std::shared_ptr<TestMultipleObserver>> observers;
    observers.reserve(observerNames.size());
    for (const auto& name : observerNames) {
        observers.push_back(std::make_shared<TestMultipleObserver>(name));
    }
    
    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Add all observers to watch target
    for (auto& observer : observers) {
        graph.addObserver(target, observer);
    }
    
    // Notify target
    target->notify();
    
    // Count notifications and verify all observers were notified
    int totalCalls = 0;
    bool allNotified = true;
    for (auto& observer : observers) {
        totalCalls += observer->getCallCount();
        if (!observer->wasChanged()) {
            allNotified = false;
        }
    }
    
    return {totalCalls, allNotified};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

#pragma once

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class TestChainedObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestChainedObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations

/**
 * Function: Test chained observer relationships
 *
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @param observer1Name Name for the first observer node
 * @param observer2Name Name for the second observer node (observes observer1)
 * @return std::pair<int, bool> Pair of total call count and whether chain notification worked correctly
 */
std::pair<int, bool> test_chained_observers(const std::string& targetName, 
                                            const std::string& observer1Name, 
                                            const std::string& observer2Name) {
    // Create observer nodes
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(targetName);
    auto observer1 = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(observer1Name);
    auto observer2 = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(observer2Name);
    
    // Reset nodes to clear any previous state
    target->reset();
    observer1->reset();
    observer2->reset();
    
    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Register nodes with the graph
    graph.addNode(target);
    graph.addNode(observer1);
    graph.addNode(observer2);
    
    // Set up chain relationships
    target->addOneObserver(observer1);
    observer1->addOneObserver(observer2);
    
    // Notify target
    target->valueChanged(true);
    
    // Verify both observers were notified
    bool observer1Notified = observer1->getCallCount() > 0;
    bool observer2Notified = observer2->getCallCount() > 0;
    
    // Calculate total call count
    int totalCalls = target->getCallCount() + observer1->getCallCount() + observer2->getCallCount();
    
    // Chain is considered working correctly if both observers were notified
    bool chainWorked = observer1Notified && observer2Notified;
    
    return {totalCalls, chainWorked};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test resetting a calculation node with new dependencies
 *
 * @param a First input value
 * @param b Second input value
 * @param c Third input value
 * @return int The result after resetting calculation to use different dependencies
 */
int test_calculation_reset(int a, int b, int c) {
    // Create reactive variables
    auto x = reaction::var(a);
    auto y = reaction::var(b);
    auto z = reaction::var(c);
    
    // Create initial calculation: sum of x and y
    auto calc = reaction::calc([&]() {
        return x.get() + y.get();
    });
    
    // Reset calculation with different dependencies: sum of y and z
    calc.reset([&]() {
        return y.get() + z.get();
    });
    
    // Return the result after reset
    return calc.get();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class PersonRecord : public reaction::FieldBase {
public:
    PersonRecord(const std::string& name, int age)
        : m_name(name), m_age(age) {}

    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name.get(); }
    int getAge() const { return m_age.get(); }

    void setName(const std::string& name) { m_name.value(name); }
    void setAge(int age) { m_age.value(age); }

private:
    reaction::Var<std::string> m_name;
    reaction::Var<int> m_age;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test reactive fields updating calculated info
 *
 * @param initialName Initial person name
 * @param initialAge Initial person age
 * @param newName Updated person name
 * @param newAge Updated person age
 * @return std::string Concatenated info after updates
 */
std::string test_reactive_fields(const std::string& initialName,
                                 int initialAge,
                                 const std::string& newName,
                                 int newAge) {
    // Create reactive PersonRecord variable
    auto person = reaction::var(PersonRecord{initialName, initialAge});
    
    // Create calculation depending on reactive fields
    auto info = reaction::calc([&person]() {
        return person.get().getName() + " is " + std::to_string(person.get().getAge()) + " years old.";
    });
    
    // Update name field
    person.get().setName(newName);
    
    // Update age field
    person.get().setAge(newAge);
    
    // Return updated info
    return info.get();
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test batch execution reduces recomputation count
 *
 * @param initialA Initial value for variable a
 * @param initialB Initial value for variable b
 * @param nonBatchA Value to set a without batching
 * @param nonBatchB Value to set b without batching
 * @param batchA Value to set a within batch
 * @param batchB Value to set b within batch
 * @return std::tuple<int, int, int> {final sum, count after non-batch updates, count after batch updates}
 */
std::tuple<int, int, int> test_batch_execution(int initialA,
                                               int initialB,
                                               int nonBatchA,
                                               int nonBatchB,
                                               int batchA,
                                               int batchB) {
    // Create reactive variables
    auto a = reaction::var(initialA);
    auto b = reaction::var(initialB);
    
    // Create variable to track recomputation count
    int recompute_count = 0;
    
    // Create calculation that depends on a and b
    auto sum_calc = reaction::calc([&]() {
        recompute_count++;
        return a() + b();
    });
    
    // Perform non-batch updates
    a.value(nonBatchA);
    b.value(nonBatchB);
    
    // Record recompute count after non-batch updates
    int count_after_non_batch = recompute_count;
    
    // Create batch context and execute batch updates
    reaction::batch([&]() {
        a.value(batchA);
        b.value(batchB);
    }).execute();
    
    // Record recompute count after batch updates
    int count_after_batch = recompute_count;
    
    // Return final sum and counts
    return std::make_tuple(sum_calc(), count_after_non_batch, count_after_batch);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test naming reactive nodes and retrieving names
 *
 * @param valueA Initial value for varA
 * @param valueB Initial value for varB
 * @return std::string Combined string of retrieved names
 */
std::string test_named_reactions(int valueA, int valueB) {
    // Create reactive variables with names
    auto varA = reaction::var(valueA, "VariableA");
    auto varB = reaction::var(valueB, "VariableB");
    
    // Create calculations with names
    auto calc = reaction::calc([](int a, int b) { return a + b; }, varA, varB, "SumCalculation");
    
    // Create action with name
    auto action = reaction::action([](int a, int b) { 
        return a * b; // Example action: multiply
    }, varA, varB, "MultiplyAction");
    
    // Retrieve names
    std::string varAName = varA.getName();
    std::string varBName = varB.getName();
    std::string calcName = calc.getName();
    std::string actionName = action.getName();
    
    // Return combined names string
    return "varA: " + varAName + ", varB: " + varBName + 
           ", calc: " + calcName + ", action: " + actionName;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test filter trigger with conditional recomputation
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial sensor value
 * @param firstUpdate First update (expected to be within threshold)
 * @param secondUpdate Second update (expected to exceed threshold)
 * @param diffThreshold Minimum delta to trigger recomputation
 * @param levelThreshold Threshold to categorize HIGH vs NORMAL
 * @return std::tuple<std::string, int, int>
 *         {final status string, recompute count after first update, recompute count after second update}
 */
std::tuple<std::string, int, int> test_filter_trigger(double initialValue,
                                                      double firstUpdate,
                                                      double secondUpdate,
                                                      double diffThreshold,
                                                      double levelThreshold) {
    // 1. Create reactive variable (sensor)
    auto sensor = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // 2. Track recomputation count and last processed value
    int recomputeCount = 0;
    double lastProcessedValue = 0.0;
    
    // 3. Create filter-triggered calculation
    auto statusCalc = reaction::calc([&]() {
        double currentValue = sensor.get();
        recomputeCount++;
        lastProcessedValue = currentValue;
        
        // Determine status based on level threshold
        if (currentValue > levelThreshold) {
            return "HIGH";
        } else {
            return "NORMAL";
        }
    });
    
    // 4. Apply filter (only trigger when delta exceeds threshold)
    statusCalc.filter([&](double newSensor, double oldSensor) {
        return std::abs(newSensor - oldSensor) > diffThreshold;
    });
    
    // 5. Update sensor with first value
    sensor.value(firstUpdate);
    
    // 6. Record recompute count after first update
    int countAfterFirst = recomputeCount;
    
    // 7. Update sensor with second value
    sensor.value(secondUpdate);
    
    // 8. Record recompute count after second update
    int countAfterSecond = recomputeCount;
    
    // 9. Get final status
    std::string finalStatus = statusCalc.get();
    
    // 10. Return results
    return std::make_tuple(finalStatus, countAfterFirst, countAfterSecond);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

class FieldOwnerNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notificationCount++;
    }
    
    int getNotificationCount() const {
        return m_notificationCount;
    }

private:
    int m_notificationCount = 0;
};

class ProfileFields : public reaction::FieldBase {
public:
    ProfileFields(int initialScore, bool initialStatus)
        : m_score(initialScore), m_active(initialStatus) {}
    
    reaction::Var<int>& score() { return m_score; }
    reaction::Var<bool>& active() { return m_active; }

private:
    reaction::Var<int> m_score;
    reaction::Var<bool> m_active;
};

struct FieldBindingResult {
    int notificationsAfterScoreUpdate;
    int notificationsAfterStatusUpdate;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementation
// ============================================

FieldBindingResult test_field_binding(int initialScore, int scoreDelta) {
    // Step 1: Create ProfileFields object
    ProfileFields profile(initialScore, true);
    
    // Step 2: Create FieldOwnerNode
    FieldOwnerNode ownerNode;
    
    // Step 3: Get observer graph instance and register owner node
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    observerGraph.registerNode(&ownerNode);
    
    // Step 4: Get field graph instance and bind fields to owner node
    auto& fieldGraph = reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance();
    fieldGraph.bindFields(profile.getId(), &ownerNode);
    
    // Step 5: Update score field and record notification count
    profile.score() += scoreDelta;
    int notificationsAfterScore = ownerNode.getNotificationCount();
    
    // Step 6: Update active field and record notification count
    profile.active() = false;
    int notificationsAfterStatus = ownerNode.getNotificationCount();
    
    // Step 7: Return notification counts
    return FieldBindingResult{notificationsAfterScore, notificationsAfterStatus};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct CreateFactoryResult {
    int initialExpressionValue;
    int updatedExpressionValue;
    int squaredValueAfterUpdate;
};

CreateFactoryResult test_reactive_create_factory(int baseValue, int increment) {
    // Create reactive variable
    auto reactiveVar = reaction::create(baseValue);
    
    // Create reactive expression: variable + increment
    auto reactiveExpr = reaction::create(reactiveVar + increment);
    
    // Create reactive calculation: variable squared
    auto reactiveCalc = reaction::create([reactiveVar]() { 
        return reactiveVar() * reactiveVar(); 
    });
    
    // Get initial expression value
    int initialExprValue = reactiveExpr();
    
    // Update reactive variable
    reactiveVar.value(baseValue + increment);
    
    // Get updated expression value
    int updatedExprValue = reactiveExpr();
    
    // Get squared value after update
    int squaredValue = reactiveCalc();
    
    return {initialExprValue, updatedExprValue, squaredValue};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

 The function must perform the following steps:
 1. Create NamedObserverNode instances.
 2. Get the observer graph instance.
 3. Register nodes.
 4. Set up direct observer relationship.
 5. Set up transitive observer relationship.

 9. Check if the transitive observer is in the collected set.
 10. Return a summary.

 We are provided with the necessary headers and the structure of the `NamedObserverNode` and `ObserverCollectionSummary`.

 Steps in code:

 1. Create NamedObserverNode instances: We'll create at least three nodes: a source, a direct observer, and a transitive observer.
 2. Get the observer graph instance: Using `reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance()`.
 3. Register nodes: We need to register each node with the graph. This is done by calling `registerNode` on the graph for each node.
 4. Set up direct observer relationship: We'll make the direct observer observe the source. This can be done by calling `addOneObserver` on the source node, passing the direct observer.
 5. Set up transitive observer relationship: We'll make the transitive observer observe the direct observer. Again, using `addOneObserver` on the direct observer, passing the transitive observer.
 6. Create a NodeSet: We'll use `reaction::NodeSet` to store the collected observers.
 7. Collect observers: We'll call `collectObservers` on the graph, passing the source node and the NodeSet.
 8. Check if direct observer is in the set: We can check using the `contains` method of NodeSet.
 9. Check if transitive observer is in the set: Similarly, check with the `contains` method.
 10. Return the summary: We'll create an `ObserverCollectionSummary` object with the total number of observers (the size of the NodeSet), and the two boolean flags.

 Note: We must include the necessary headers. However, the problem says to output the complete contents of the file, so we must include the given code and then implement the function.

 Since the function is in the namespace `test_reaction`, we must implement it within that namespace.

 Let's write the code accordingly.
output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

class NamedObserverNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    explicit NamedObserverNode(std::string name) : m_name(std::move(name)) {
    }
    
    const std::string& name() const {
        return m_name;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
};

struct ObserverCollectionSummary {
    std::size_t totalObservers;
    bool containsDirectObserver;
    bool containsTransitiveObserver;
};

ObserverCollectionSummary test_observer_collection() {
    // Create nodes
    auto source = std::make_shared<NamedObserverNode>("source");
    auto direct_observer = std::make_shared<NamedObserverNode>("direct");
    auto transitive_observer = std::make_shared<NamedObserverNode>("transitive");
    
    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Register nodes
    graph.registerNode(source);
    graph.registerNode(direct_observer);
    graph.registerNode(transitive_observer);
    
    // Set up direct observer relationship
    source->addOneObserver(direct_observer);
    
    // Set up transitive observer relationship
    direct_observer->addOneObserver(transitive_observer);
    
    // Create NodeSet to store collected observers
    reaction::NodeSet observers;
    
    // Collect observers from source
    graph.collectObservers(source, observers);
    
    // Check if direct observer is in collected set
    bool has_direct = observers.contains(direct_observer);
    
    // Check if transitive observer is in collected set
    bool has_transitive = observers.contains(transitive_observer);
    
    // Return collection summary
    return ObserverCollectionSummary{
        .totalObservers = observers.size(),
        .containsDirectObserver = has_direct,
        .containsTransitiveObserver = has_transitive
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.reasoning:
<think>We are given the function signature and the expected behavior. We need to implement the function using the `reaction` library.

Steps:
1. Get the thread manager instance using `reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance()`.
2. Reset the thread manager state using `resetForTesting()`.
3. Register the current thread (the main thread) by calling `registerThread()` on the thread manager.
4. Get the thread count and safety status before creating the worker thread. We can get the safety status with `isThreadSafetyEnabled()` and the thread count with `getThreadCount()`.
5. Create a worker thread that also registers itself by calling `reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance().registerThread()`. We'll use a lambda for the thread function.
6. Wait for the worker thread to finish.
7. Get the thread count and safety status again after the worker thread has been joined.
8. Return a `ThreadSafetyStatus` struct with the collected data.

Note: We must ensure that the worker thread is properly created and joined.

Let's write the code accordingly.
output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ThreadSafetyStatus {
    bool safetyBefore;
    bool safetyAfter;
    std::size_t threadCountBefore;
    std::size_t threadCountAfter;
};

ThreadSafetyStatus test_thread_manager_multithread() {
    // 1. Get thread manager instance
    auto& threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // 2. Reset thread manager state
    threadManager.resetForTesting();
    
    // 3. Register current thread
    threadManager.registerThread();
    
    // 4. Get thread count and safety status before
    bool safetyBefore = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    std::size_t threadCountBefore = threadManager.getThreadCount();
    
    // 5. Create and start worker thread
    std::thread worker([&threadManager]() {
        // Worker thread registers itself
        threadManager.registerThread();
    });
    
    // 6. Wait for worker thread
    worker.join();
    
    // 7. Get thread count and safety status after
    bool safetyAfter = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    std::size_t threadCountAfter = threadManager.getThreadCount();
    
    // 8. Return thread safety status
    return ThreadSafetyStatus{
        safetyBefore,
        safetyAfter,
        threadCountBefore,
        threadCountAfter
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct BatchExecuteResult {
    int recomputeCountBeforeBatch;
    int recomputeCountAfterBatch;
    int finalValue;
};

BatchExecuteResult test_batch_execute_update(int baseValue, int deltaA, int deltaB) {
    // Create reactive variable
    auto reactiveVar = reaction::var(baseValue);
    
    // Create variable to track recomputation count (non-reactive local variable)
    int recomputeCount = 0;
    
    // Create calculation that depends on reactiveVar and increments recomputeCount
    auto calc = reaction::calc([&]() {
        recomputeCount++;
        return reactiveVar.get();
    });
    
    // Record recompute count before batch (initial evaluation)
    int recomputeCountBeforeBatch = recomputeCount;
    
    // Execute batch updates
    reaction::batchExecute([&]() {
        reactiveVar.value(baseValue + deltaA);
        reactiveVar.value(baseValue + deltaA + deltaB);
    });
    
    // Record recompute count after batch
    int recomputeCountAfterBatch = recomputeCount;
    
    // Get final value
    int finalValue = reactiveVar.get();
    
    return BatchExecuteResult{
        recomputeCountBeforeBatch,
        recomputeCountAfterBatch,
        finalValue
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

class CacheProbeNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        // Do nothing for testing.
    }
};

struct CacheStatsSnapshot {
    size_t graphEntriesBeforeCleanup;
    size_t graphEntriesAfterCleanup;
    double graphHitRatioAfterCleanup;
};

CacheStatsSnapshot test_observer_cache_stats() {
    // Create CacheProbeNode instances
    auto node1 = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();
    auto node2 = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();
    auto node3 = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();
    
    // Get observer graph instance
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Register nodes
    observerGraph.registerNode(node1);
    observerGraph.registerNode(node2);
    observerGraph.registerNode(node3);
    
    // Set up direct observer relationship
    node1->observe(node2);
    
    // Set up transitive observer relationship
    node2->observe(node3);
    
    // Create NodeSet to store collected observers
    reaction::NodeSet observers;
    
    // Populate cache data by collecting observers
    observerGraph.getObservers(node1, observers);
    
    // Get cache statistics before cleanup
    auto cacheStatsBefore = observerGraph.getCacheStats();
    
    // Trigger cache cleanup
    observerGraph.triggerCacheCleanup();
    
    // Get cache statistics after cleanup
    auto cacheStatsAfter = observerGraph.getCacheStats();
    
    // Close all nodes
    node1->close();
    node2->close();
    node3->close();
    
    // Return cache stats snapshot
    return CacheStatsSnapshot{
        cacheStatsBefore.graphStats.totalEntries,
        cacheStatsAfter.graphStats.totalEntries,
        cacheStatsAfter.graphStats.hitRatio
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

  - We'll create a mutable variable (say `mutableVar`) with the initial value `baseValue`.
  - We'll create a constant variable (say `constVar`) with the value `constValue`.
  - We'll create a calculation (say `sumCalc`) that returns the sum of `mutableVar` and `constVar`.
  - We'll get the initial sum by calling `sumCalc.get()`.
  - Then we update the mutable variable by calling `mutableVar.value(baseValue + delta)`.
  - Then we get the updated sum by calling `sumCalc.get()` again.
  - Return the `ConstVarResult` with the two sums.

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ConstVarResult {
    int initialSum;
    int updatedSum;
};

ConstVarResult test_const_var_propagation(int baseValue, int constValue, int delta) {
    // Create a mutable reactive variable
    auto mutableVar = reaction::var(baseValue);
    
    // Create a constant reactive variable
    auto constVar = reaction::constVar(constValue);
    
    // Create a calculation that sums both variables
    auto sumCalc = reaction::calc([&]() {
        return mutableVar.get() + constVar.get();
    });
    
    // Get the initial sum
    int initialSum = sumCalc.get();
    
    // Update the mutable reactive variable
    mutableVar.value(baseValue + delta);
    
    // Get the updated sum
    int updatedSum = sumCalc.get();
    
    return ConstVarResult{initialSum, updatedSum};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct AlwaysTriggerResult {
    int recomputeCountAfterInit;
    int recomputeCountAfterDuplicateWrite;

AlwaysTriggerResult test_always_trigger(int initialValue) {

    auto reactiveVar = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // Create a counter to track recomputation count
    int recomputeCount = 0;
    
    // Create an AlwaysTrig calculation that doubles the value and increments counter
    auto calcResult = reaction::calc<reaction::AlwaysTrig>([&reactiveVar, &recomputeCount]() {
        recomputeCount++;
        return reactiveVar() * 2;
    });
    
    // Store recompute count after initialization
    int afterInit = recomputeCount;
    
    // Write the same value again
    reactiveVar.value(initialValue);
    
    // Store recompute count after duplicate write
    int afterDuplicateWrite = recomputeCount;
    
    // Force evaluation to avoid unused variable warnings
    calcResult.get();
    
    return AlwaysTriggerResult{afterInit, afterDuplicateWrite};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

 We are provided with the necessary headers and the implementation of the ThreadManager and ThreadRegistrationGuard.

 Important: The ThreadRegistrationGuard is used in the worker thread to register it. The ThreadManager will detect multiple threads and enable thread safety.

 Let's break down the code:

 - We include the necessary headers and define the namespace.
 - We create the ThreadGuardState struct as given.
 - We implement the function `test_thread_guard`.

 We note that the ThreadManager is a singleton, so we can get the instance via `reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance()`.

 We reset the state of the ThreadManager to start from a clean slate.

 Then we record the safety status and thread count before creating the worker thread.

 We then create a worker thread that runs a lambda which constructs a ThreadRegistrationGuard. This will cause the worker thread to be registered.

 We wait for the worker thread to finish.

 Then we record the safety status and thread count again.

 Finally, we return the ThreadGuardState struct.

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <thread>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ThreadGuardState {
    bool safetyBeforeGuard;
    bool safetyAfterGuard;
    std::size_t threadCountBeforeGuard;
    std::size_t threadCountAfterGuard;
};

ThreadGuardState test_thread_guard() {
    // 1. Get thread manager instance
    auto& threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // 2. Reset thread manager state
    threadManager.resetForTesting();
    
    // 3. Get thread safety status before guard
    bool safetyBeforeGuard = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // 4. Get thread count before guard
    std::size_t threadCountBeforeGuard = threadManager.getThreadCount();
    
    // 5. Create a worker thread with ThreadRegistrationGuard
    std::thread worker([&]() {
        // ThreadRegistrationGuard will automatically register the thread
        reaction::ThreadRegistrationGuard guard;
    });
    
    // 6. Wait for worker thread to complete
    worker.join();
    
    // 7. Get thread safety status after guard
    bool safetyAfterGuard = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // 8. Get thread count after guard
    std::size_t threadCountAfterGuard = threadManager.getThreadCount();
    
    // 9. Return thread guard state
    return ThreadGuardState{
        safetyBeforeGuard,
        safetyAfterGuard,
        threadCountBeforeGuard,
        threadCountAfterGuard
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

class IdTrackedFields : public reaction::FieldBase {
public:
    explicit IdTrackedFields(int value) : m_valueField(reaction::var(value)) {
    }
    
    reaction::Var<int>& valueField() {
        return m_valueField;
    }

private:
    reaction::Var<int> m_valueField;
};

struct FieldIdComparison {
    uint64_t firstIdFirstRead;
    uint64_t firstIdSecondRead;
    uint64_t secondId;
};

FieldIdComparison test_field_unique_id(int initialFirst, int initialSecond) {
    // Create first and second instances
    IdTrackedFields first(initialFirst);
    IdTrackedFields second(initialSecond);
    
    // Read IDs
    uint64_t firstIdFirst = first.getId();
    uint64_t firstIdSecond = first.getId();
    uint64_t secondId = second.getId();
    
    // Update reactive fields to avoid unused variable warnings
    first.valueField().value(initialFirst + 1);
    second.valueField().value(initialSecond + 1);
    
    // Return comparison
    return FieldIdComparison{firstIdFirst, firstIdSecond, secondId};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

 - We get the initial value of the calculation.
 - Then update the source variable to a new value.
 - Get the updated value of the calculation.
 - Close the source variable (which will break the dependency).
 - Then get the value of the calculation again, which should be the last known value (the doubled updated value) because of the LastHandle policy.

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct LastHandleSnapshot {
    int initialValue;
    int afterUpdate;
    int afterSourceClose;
};

LastHandleSnapshot test_last_handle_restore(int initialValue, int updatedValue) {
    // Create a reactive variable
    auto sourceVar = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // Create a calculation with LastHandle policy
    auto calcWithLastHandle = reaction::calc<reaction::LastHandle>([&]() {
        return sourceVar() * 2; // Example calculation: double the value
    });
    
    // Get the initial result
    int initialResult = calcWithLastHandle();
    
    // Update the source variable
    sourceVar.value(updatedValue);
    
    // Get the updated result
    int updatedResult = calcWithLastHandle();
    
    // Close the source node
    sourceVar.close();
    
    // Get the result after source close
    int resultAfterClose = calcWithLastHandle();
    
    // Return the snapshot
    return LastHandleSnapshot{
        .initialValue = initialResult,
        .afterUpdate = updatedResult,
        .afterSourceClose = resultAfterClose
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/id_generator.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct UniqueIdSequence {
    uint64_t firstValue;
    uint64_t secondValue;
    uint64_t thirdValue;
    bool strictlyIncreasing;
};

UniqueIdSequence test_unique_id_sequence() {
    // Create three UniqueID instances
    reaction::UniqueID id1;
    reaction::UniqueID id2;
    reaction::UniqueID id3;
    
    // Get values from each instance
    uint64_t first = id1.getValue();
    uint64_t second = id2.getValue();
    uint64_t third = id3.getValue();
    
    // Check if the sequence is strictly increasing
    bool increasing = (first < second) && (second < third);
    
    // Return the sequence with the values and the check result
    return UniqueIdSequence{first, second, third, increasing};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/resource.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ResourceAtomicResult {
    int finalValue;
    bool operationReportedChange;
};

ResourceAtomicResult test_resource_atomic(int initialValue, int delta) {
    // Create a Resource instance with initial value
    reaction::Resource<int> resource(initialValue);
    
    // Variable to track if operation reported a change
    bool changeReported = false;
    
    // Perform atomic operation that adds delta to the value
    resource.atomicOperation([&delta](int& val) -> bool {
        val += delta;
        return true; // Change is always reported when we modify the value
    }, changeReported);
    
    // Get the final value
    int finalValue = resource.get();
    
    // Return the result
    return ResourceAtomicResult{finalValue, changeReported};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <cstddef>
#include <reaction/memory/stack_monitor.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct StackMonitorReport {
    bool monitoringEnabledBefore;
    bool guardAllocated;
    std::size_t usageDuringGuard;
    std::size_t usageAfterGuard;
    double usagePercentage;
    bool nearLimitDuringGuard;
    bool monitoringAfterDisable;
};

StackMonitorReport test_stack_monitor_guard() {
    StackMonitorReport report{};
    
    // Step 1: Disable monitoring
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::disableMonitoring();
    
    // Step 2: Initialize stack monitor
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::initialize();
    
    // Step 3: Create report struct (done above)
    
    // Step 4: Check if monitoring is enabled before guard
    report.monitoringEnabledBefore = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::isMonitoringEnabled();
    
    // Step 5: Create StackGuard in a scope block
    bool guardAllocated = false;
    std::size_t usageDuring = 0;
    double percentage = 0.0;
    bool nearLimit = false;
    
    {
        reaction::memory::StackMonitor::StackGuard<64> guard; // Using a fixed size for testing
        
        // Step 6: Check if guard was successfully allocated
        guardAllocated = guard.allocated;
        
        if (guardAllocated) {
            // Step 7: Get current usage during guard
            usageDuring = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getCurrentUsage();
            
            // Step 8: Get statistics
            auto stats = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getStats();
            percentage = stats.usagePercentage;
            nearLimit = stats.nearLimit;
        }
    } // Guard goes out of scope here
    
    // Step 10: Exit guard scope (handled by block)
    
    // Step 11: Get current usage after guard
    std::size_t usageAfter = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getCurrentUsage();
    
    // Step 12: Disable monitoring again
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::disableMonitoring();
    
    // Step 13: Check if monitoring is enabled after disable
    report.monitoringAfterDisable = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::isMonitoringEnabled();
    
    // Populate report
    report.guardAllocated = guardAllocated;
    report.usageDuringGuard = usageDuring;
    report.usageAfterGuard = usageAfter;
    report.usagePercentage = percentage;
    report.nearLimitDuringGuard = nearLimit;
    
    return report;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <reaction/core/react.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ConditionalMutexResult {
    bool tryLockWithoutSafety;
    bool tryLockWithSafety;
};

ConditionalMutexResult test_conditional_mutex_behavior() {
    // 1. Get thread manager instance
    reaction::ThreadManager& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // 2. Reset thread manager state
    tm.resetForTesting();
    
    // 3. Create ConditionalMutex instance
    reaction::ConditionalMutex mutex;
    
    // 4. Create ConditionalLockGuard in a scope block (without safety enabled)
    bool locked_without_safety = false;
    {
        reaction::ConditionalLockGuard<reaction::ConditionalMutex> guard(mutex);
        // 5. Attempt to lock mutex without safety enabled - success expected
        locked_without_safety = true; // Guard successfully locks when thread safety is disabled
    } // 6. Exit guard scope
    
    // 7. Enable thread safety
    tm.enableThreadSafety();
    
    // 8. Create ConditionalLockGuard in a scope block again (with safety enabled)
    bool locked_with_safety = false;
    {
        reaction::ConditionalLockGuard<reaction::ConditionalMutex> guard(mutex);
        // 9. Attempt to lock mutex with safety enabled - success expected
        locked_with_safety = true; // Guard successfully locks when thread safety is enabled
    } // 10. Exit guard scope
    
    // 11. Return conditional mutex result
    return {locked_without_safety, locked_with_safety};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <reaction/core/conditional_mutex.h>  // For ConditionalSharedLockGuard

namespace test_reaction {

struct SharedMutexResult {
    bool trySharedWithoutSafety;
    bool tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety;
    bool trySharedWithSafety;
    bool tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety;
};

SharedMutexResult test_conditional_shared_mutex() {
    // Step 1: Get thread manager instance
    auto& tm = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // Step 2: Reset thread manager state
    tm.resetForTesting();
    
    // Step 3: Create ConditionalSharedMutex instance
    reaction::ConditionalSharedMutex mutex;
    
    // Step 4: Attempt to acquire shared lock without safety
    bool trySharedWithoutSafety = mutex.try_lock_shared();
    
    // Step 5: Unlock shared lock if acquired
    if (trySharedWithoutSafety) {
        mutex.unlock_shared();
    }
    
    // Step 6: Create ConditionalSharedLockGuard in a scope block
    {
        reaction::ConditionalSharedLockGuard<reaction::ConditionalSharedMutex> guard(mutex);
        
        // Step 7: Attempt to acquire unique lock while shared lock is held without safety
        bool tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety = mutex.try_lock();
        
        // Step 8: Unlock unique lock if acquired
        if (tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety) {
            mutex.unlock();
        }
    } // Step 9: Exit guard scope
    
    // Step 10: Enable thread safety
    tm.enableThreadSafety();
    
    // Step 11: Attempt to acquire shared lock with safety
    bool trySharedWithSafety = mutex.try_lock_shared();
    
    // Step 12: Unlock shared lock if acquired
    if (trySharedWithSafety) {
        mutex.unlock_shared();
    }
    
    // Step 13: Create ConditionalSharedLockGuard in a scope block again
    {
        reaction::ConditionalSharedLockGuard<reaction::ConditionalSharedMutex> guard(mutex);
        
        // Step 14: Attempt to acquire unique lock while shared lock is held with safety
        bool tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety = mutex.try_lock();
        
        // Step 15: Unlock unique lock if acquired
        if (tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety) {
            mutex.unlock();
        }
    } // Step 16: Exit guard scope
    
    // Step 17: Return shared mutex result
    return SharedMutexResult{
        trySharedWithoutSafety,
        tryUniqueWhileSharedWithoutSafety,
        trySharedWithSafety,
        tryUniqueWhileSharedWithSafety
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/memory/memory_config.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct TinyPod {
    int a;
    int b;
};

struct MediumPod {
    double values[3];
};

struct LargePod {
    std::byte payload[128];
};

struct SboTraitSummary {
    bool tinyAlwaysSbo;
    bool tinyForceHeap;
    bool mediumConditionalSbo;
    bool mediumForceHeap;
    bool largeForceHeap;
    std::size_t alwaysThreshold;
    std::size_t conditionalThreshold;
    std::size_t maxStackUsage;
};

SboTraitSummary analyze_sbo_traits() {
    SboTraitSummary summary;
    
    // Get SBO traits for TinyPod
    summary.tinyAlwaysSbo = reaction::memory::SBOTraits<TinyPod>::always_sbo;
    summary.tinyForceHeap = reaction::memory::SBOTraits<TinyPod>::force_heap;
    
    // Get SBO traits for MediumPod
    summary.mediumConditionalSbo = reaction::memory::SBOTraits<MediumPod>::conditional_sbo;
    summary.mediumForceHeap = reaction::memory::SBOTraits<MediumPod>::force_heap;
    
    // Get SBO traits for LargePod
    summary.largeForceHeap = reaction::memory::SBOTraits<LargePod>::force_heap;
    
    // Get SBO configuration thresholds
    auto config = reaction::memory::get_sbo_config();
    summary.alwaysThreshold = config.always_threshold;
    summary.conditionalThreshold = config.conditional_threshold;
    summary.maxStackUsage = config.max_stack_usage;
    
    return summary;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ResetBehaviorSnapshot {
    int beforeResetValue;
    int afterResetValue;
    bool safetyEnabledDuringReset;
};

ResetBehaviorSnapshot test_react_reset_behavior(int initialValue, int multiplier) {
    // Step 1: Get thread manager instance
    auto& threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // Step 2: Reset thread manager state
    threadManager.resetForTesting();
    
    // Step 3: Create a reactive variable
    auto reactiveVar = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // Step 4: Create a calculation
    auto calculation = reaction::calc([&reactiveVar, multiplier]() {
        return reactiveVar() * multiplier;
    });
    
    // Step 5: Get value before reset
    int beforeResetValue = calculation.get();
    
    // Step 6: Enable thread safety
    threadManager.enableThreadSafety();
    
    // Step 7: Check if thread safety is enabled
    bool safetyEnabled = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // Step 8: Reset calculation logic
    calculation.reset([&reactiveVar, multiplier]() {
        return reactiveVar() * (multiplier + 1); // Change multiplier to multiplier+1
    });
    
    // Step 9: Update source variable
    reactiveVar.value(initialValue + 1);
    
    // Step 10: Get value after reset
    int afterResetValue = calculation.get();
    
    // Step 11: Return reset behavior snapshot
    return ResetBehaviorSnapshot{
        .beforeResetValue = beforeResetValue,
        .afterResetValue = afterResetValue,
        .safetyEnabledDuringReset = safetyEnabled
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

 Implementation:

 We'll create a vector of strings, reserve space equal to the size of the input vector to avoid multiple allocations.
 Then, for each error code in the input vector, we convert it to a string using the static member function `reaction::ReactionException::errorCodeToString`.

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/exception.h>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

std::vector<std::string> describe_error_codes(
    const std::vector<reaction::ReactionException::ErrorCode> &codes) {
    std::vector<std::string> descriptions;
    descriptions.reserve(codes.size());
    
    for (const auto& code : codes) {
        descriptions.push_back(reaction::ReactionException::errorCodeToString(code));
    }
    
    return descriptions;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/exception.h>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ExceptionInfo {
    std::string formattedMessage;
    std::string originalMessage;
    std::string file;
    int line;
    std::string function;
    std::string codeName;
};

ExceptionInfo capture_exception_info(const std::string &message,
    reaction::ReactionException::ErrorCode code,
    const std::string &file,
    int line,
    const std::string &function) {
    // Create ReactionException instance
    reaction::ReactionException exception(message, code, file, line, function);
    
    // Populate ExceptionInfo struct
    ExceptionInfo info;
    info.formattedMessage = exception.getFormattedMessage();
    info.originalMessage = exception.getOriginalMessage();
    info.file = exception.getFile();
    info.line = exception.getLine();
    info.function = exception.getFunction();
    info.codeName = reaction::ReactionException::errorCodeToString(code);
    
    return info;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct PointerAccessSnapshot {
    int valueAfterFirstAdd;
    int valueAfterSecondAdd;
    bool pointerStable;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate pointer-style access for reactive structs.
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value stored inside the Counter
 * @param delta Amount added on each pointer invocation
 * @return PointerAccessSnapshot Snapshot capturing results after pointer operations
 */
inline PointerAccessSnapshot test_react_pointer_access(int initialValue, int delta) {
    // Define Counter struct
    struct Counter {
        int value;
        void add(int delta) { value += delta; }
    };
    
    // Create reactive variable with Counter
    auto reactive_counter = reaction::var(Counter{initialValue});
    
    // Get first pointer
    auto first_ptr = reactive_counter.getPtr();
    
    // Call add method through pointer
    first_ptr->add(delta);
    
    // Get current value after first add
    int value_after_first = first_ptr->value;
    
    // Get second pointer
    auto second_ptr = reactive_counter.getPtr();
    
    // Call add method through pointer again
    second_ptr->add(delta);
    
    // Get current value after second add
    int value_after_second = second_ptr->value;
    
    // Check if pointer is stable (both pointers should be the same instance)
    bool is_stable = (first_ptr == second_ptr);
    
    return PointerAccessSnapshot{
        value_after_first,
        value_after_second,
        is_stable
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ValiditySnapshot {
    bool firstValidBeforeClose;
    bool firstValidAfterClose;
    bool copiesEqualBeforeClose;
    bool copiesEqualAfterClose;
    bool equalityCheckFailedAfterClose;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate React::operator bool and operator== for validity tracking.
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value stored inside the reactive variable
 * @return ValiditySnapshot Snapshot describing validity/equality before and after close
 */
inline ValiditySnapshot test_react_validity(int initialValue) {
    // Create reactive variable
    auto source = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // Create copy of source
    auto copy = source;
    
    // Check validity before close
    bool firstValidBeforeClose = static_cast<bool>(source);
    
    // Check equality before close
    bool copiesEqualBeforeClose = (source == copy);
    
    // Close source node
    source.close();
    
    // Check validity after close
    bool firstValidAfterClose = static_cast<bool>(source);
    
    // Check equality after close with exception handling
    bool copiesEqualAfterClose = false;
    bool equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = false;
    
    try {
        copiesEqualAfterClose = (source == copy);
    } catch (...) {
        equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = true;
    }
    
    return ValiditySnapshot{
        firstValidBeforeClose,
        firstValidAfterClose,
        copiesEqualBeforeClose,
        copiesEqualAfterClose,
        equalityCheckFailedAfterClose
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <set>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

struct OrderingSnapshot {
    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    bool allUnique;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate React::operator< for ordering reactive handles.
 *
 * @param firstName Name assigned to the first reactive node
 * @param secondName Name assigned to the second reactive node
 * @param thirdName Name assigned to the third reactive node
 * @return OrderingSnapshot Snapshot of sorted names and uniqueness flag
 */
inline OrderingSnapshot test_react_ordering(const std::string &firstName,
                                            const std::string &secondName,
                                            const std::string &thirdName) {
    // Create reactive variables and set names
    auto var1 = reaction::var(0).setName(firstName);
    auto var2 = reaction::var(0).setName(secondName);
    auto var3 = reaction::var(0).setName(thirdName);
    
    // Insert into set to order by React::operator<
    std::set<reaction::React> reactSet;
    reactSet.insert(var1);
    reactSet.insert(var2);
    reactSet.insert(var3);
    
    // Collect ordered names
    std::vector<std::string> orderedNames;
    orderedNames.reserve(reactSet.size());
    for (const auto &react : reactSet) {
        orderedNames.push_back(react.getName());
    }
    
    // Check if all names are unique (should be true if set ordering worked correctly)
    bool allUnique = (reactSet.size() == orderedNames.size());
    
    return OrderingSnapshot{orderedNames, allUnique};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

class TrackingFieldNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    explicit TrackingFieldNode(std::string name) : m_name(name) {}
    
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notifications++;
    }
    
    int notifications() const { return m_notifications; }
    
    void clear() { m_notifications = 0; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    int m_notifications = 0;
};

struct FieldGraphCleanupResult {
    bool notifiedBeforeDelete;
    bool notifiedAfterDelete;
};

inline FieldGraphCleanupResult test_field_graph_cleanup(uint64_t objectId) {
    // Step 1: Create owner and field nodes
    auto owner = std::make_shared<TrackingFieldNode>("owner");
    auto field = std::make_shared<TrackingFieldNode>("field");
    
    // Step 2: Get observer graph instance and register nodes
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    observerGraph.addNode(owner);
    observerGraph.addNode(field);
    
    // Step 3: Get field graph instance
    auto& fieldGraph = reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance();
    
    // Step 4: Register field under object ID
    fieldGraph.addObjField(objectId, field);
    
    // Step 5: Bind field to owner
    owner->bindField(field, objectId);
    
    // Step 6: Trigger notification on field
    field->notify();
    
    // Step 7: Check if owner was notified before delete
    bool notifiedBeforeDelete = (owner->notifications() > 0);
    
    // Step 8: Clear owner notification count
    owner->clear();
    
    // Step 9: Clear dependencies on owner
    observerGraph.clearNode(owner);
    
    // Step 10: Remove field mappings for object ID
    fieldGraph.deleteObj(objectId);
    
    // Step 11: Attempt to bind field again
    owner->bindField(field, objectId);
    
    // Step 12: Trigger notification on field again
    field->notify();
    
    // Step 13: Check if owner was notified after delete
    bool notifiedAfterDelete = (owner->notifications() > 0);
    
    // Step 14: Close nodes
    owner->close();
    field->close();
    
    // Step 15: Return cleanup result
    return FieldGraphCleanupResult{notifiedBeforeDelete, notifiedAfterDelete};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/memory/memory_config.h>
#include <reaction/memory/stack_monitor.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct StackLimitSnapshot {
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::UsageStats statsBeforeGuard;
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::UsageStats statsAfterGuard;
    bool templateAllocationAllowed;
    bool runtimeAllocationAllowed;
    bool guardHeld;
    bool allocationBlockedWhileGuardHeld;
};

StackLimitSnapshot test_stack_monitor_limits() {
    // Step 1: Enable monitoring
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::enableMonitoring();
    
    // Step 2: Initialize stack monitor
    reaction::memory::StackMonitor::initialize();
    
    // Step 3: Get usage statistics before guard
    auto statsBeforeGuard = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getStats();
    
    // Step 4: Check if template allocation is allowed
    constexpr size_t LargeSize = 128;
    bool templateAllowed = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::canAllocate<LargeSize>();
    
    // Step 5: Check if runtime allocation is allowed
    bool runtimeAllowed = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::canAllocateAtRuntime(LargeSize);
    
    // Step 6: Create large StackGuard instance
    auto stackGuard = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::StackGuard<LargeSize>();
    
    // Step 7: Check if guard succeeded
    bool guardHeld = stackGuard.success();
    
    // Step 8: Check if allocation blocked while guard is held
    bool allocationBlocked = false;
    if (guardHeld) {
        // Try to allocate a small block while guard is held
        allocationBlocked = !reaction::memory::StackMonitor::canAllocateAtRuntime(1);
    }
    
    // Step 9: Get usage statistics after guard
    auto statsAfterGuard = reaction::memory::StackMonitor::getStats();
    
    // Step 10: Return stack limit snapshot
    return StackLimitSnapshot{
        .statsBeforeGuard = statsBeforeGuard,
        .statsAfterGuard = statsAfterGuard,
        .templateAllocationAllowed = templateAllowed,
        .runtimeAllocationAllowed = runtimeAllowed,
        .guardHeld = guardHeld,
        .allocationBlockedWhileGuardHeld = allocationBlocked
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/resource.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <type_traits>

namespace test_reaction {

struct VoidResourceSnapshot {
    bool retrievedIsVoid;
    bool usesSboStorage;
    bool nameAssigned;
    bool nameClearedAfterClose;
};

inline VoidResourceSnapshot test_resource_void_behavior(const std::string &nodeName) {
    // 1. Create shared Resource<Void> instance
    auto resource = reaction::Resource<Void>::create();
    
    // 2. Check if resource uses SBO storage
    bool uses_sbo = resource->isUsingSBO();
    
    // 3. Get value from resource
    auto value = resource->getValue();
    
    // 4. Check if retrieved value is Void type
    bool is_void = std::is_same_v<decltype(value), reaction::Void>;
    
    // 5. Get observer graph instance
    auto& observer_graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 6. Register resource node
    observer_graph.addNode(resource->shared_from_this());
    
    // 7. Assign node name
    observer_graph.setName(resource->shared_from_this(), nodeName);
    
    // 8. Check if name was assigned
    bool name_assigned = (observer_graph.getName(resource->shared_from_this()) == nodeName);
    
    // 9. Close node
    observer_graph.closeNode(resource->shared_from_this());
    
    // 10. Check if name was cleared after close
    bool name_cleared = (observer_graph.getName(resource->shared_from_this()).empty());
    
    // 11. Return void resource snapshot
    return VoidResourceSnapshot{
        .retrievedIsVoid = is_void,
        .usesSboStorage = uses_sbo,
        .nameAssigned = name_assigned,
        .nameClearedAfterClose = name_cleared
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

class CountingObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notifications++;
    }
    
    int notifications() const {
        return m_notifications;
    }

private:
    int m_notifications = 0;
};

struct ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot {
    int firstNotificationsAfterInitial;
    int firstNotificationsAfterUpdate;
    int secondNotificationsAfterUpdate;
    int thirdNotificationsAfterUpdate;
};

ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot test_observer_node_links() {
    auto counter = std::make_shared<CountingObserver>();
    auto source1 = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    auto source2 = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    auto source3 = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    graph.addNode(counter);
    graph.addNode(source1);
    graph.addNode(source2);
    graph.addNode(source3);
    
    source1->updateObservers({counter});
    
    source1->notify();
    
    int firstCount = counter->notifications();
    
    source1->updateObservers({});
    source2->updateObservers({counter});
    
    source1->notify();
    int firstAfterUpdate = counter->notifications();
    
    source2->notify();
    int secondAfterUpdate = counter->notifications();
    
    source3->notify();
    int thirdAfterUpdate = counter->notifications();
    
    source1->close();
    source2->close();
    source3->close();
    counter->close();
    
    return ObserverNodeLinkSnapshot{
        firstCount,
        firstAfterUpdate,
        secondAfterUpdate,
        thirdAfterUpdate
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

 The function must perform a series of steps involving creating nodes, updating their depths, and comparing them using BatchCompare.

 1. Define DepthProbeNode class (which is an ObserverNode) that has a method to set its depth.
 2. Get the thread manager instance and reset its state.
 3. Enable thread safety.
 4. Create two DepthProbeNode instances: one for shallow and one for deep.
 5. Create NodeWeak from these nodes (by calling getPtr() on them).
 6. Create a BatchCompare instance.
 7. Compare the initial nodes (shallow and deep) using BatchCompare and record the result.
 8. Update the depths of both nodes (shallow to a lower depth than deep).
 9. Compare shallow before deep (should be true because shallow has lower depth).
 10. Update the depth of shallow again to a higher depth than deep.
 11. Compare deep before shallow (should be true because deep now has lower depth).
 12. Create a null NodeWeak.
 13. Compare null with shallow (null should come first).
 14. Return a DepthOrderingSnapshot with the results.

 Note: We are using the reaction library, so we must include the necessary headers and use the provided functions.

 However, note that the provided code snippet already includes some headers and the namespace. We are to complete the function.

 Let's break down the implementation:

 1. Define DepthProbeNode: We can create a struct that inherits from ObserverNode and has a method to set the depth.
    But note: the updateDepth method is already in ObserverNode. So we can just use that.

 2. We need to get the ThreadManager instance and reset it. Then enable thread safety.

 3. We create two DepthProbeNode instances. We can use std::make_shared to create them.

 4. We get the NodeWeak from each node by calling getPtr().

 5. We create a BatchCompare instance.

 6. We compare the initial nodes (without having set any depth) - the default depth is 0? But note: the updateDepth method sets the depth to the provided value. If we don't set, then the depth is 0.

 7. Then we set the depth of shallow to 1 and deep to 2.

 8. Then we compare shallow and deep (shallow should come before deep because 1 < 2).

 9. Then we set the depth of shallow to 3 (so now deep is 2 and shallow is 3 -> deep comes first).

 10. Then we compare deep and shallow (deep should come first).

 11. Then we create a null NodeWeak (by default constructing or by resetting?).

 12. Compare null with shallow: null should come first.

 13. Return the snapshot.

 Important: We must use the reaction library functions as specified.

 However, note that the function is inline and returns a DepthOrderingSnapshot.

 We are also given the implementation of BatchCompare and the methods of ObserverNode.

 Let's code accordingly.
output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/batch.h>
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

struct DepthOrderingSnapshot {
    bool initialComparatorResult;
    bool shallowBeforeDeep;
    bool deepBeforeShallow;
    bool nullComesFirst;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate ObserverNode::updateDepth cooperating with BatchCompare ordering.
 *
 * @return DepthOrderingSnapshot Comparator outcomes after adjusting node depths.
 */
inline DepthOrderingSnapshot test_observer_depth_ordering() {
    // Step 1: Define DepthProbeNode class (inherits from ObserverNode)
    struct DepthProbeNode : reaction::ObserverNode {
        void setDepth(uint16_t depth) {
            this->updateDepth(depth);
        }
    };
    
    // Step 2: Get thread manager instance
    auto& threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // Step 3: Reset thread manager state
    threadManager.resetForTesting();
    
    // Step 4: Enable thread safety
    threadManager.enableThreadSafety();
    
    // Step 5: Create shallow and deep DepthProbeNode instances
    auto shallowNode = std::make_shared<DepthProbeNode>();
    auto deepNode = std::make_shared<DepthProbeNode>();
    
    // Step 6: Create NodeWeak from nodes
    auto shallowWeak = shallowNode->getPtr();
    auto deepWeak = deepNode->getPtr();
    
    // Step 7: Create BatchCompare instance
    reaction::BatchCompare batchCompare;
    
    // Step 8: Compare initial nodes (depths are 0 by default)
    bool initialResult = batchCompare(shallowWeak, deepWeak);
    
    // Step 9: Update depths of nodes
    shallowNode->setDepth(1);  // Shallow depth = 1
    deepNode->setDepth(2);     // Deep depth = 2
    
    // Step 10: Compare shallow before deep
    bool shallowBefore = batchCompare(shallowWeak, deepWeak);
    
    // Step 11: Update depth of shallow node again
    shallowNode->setDepth(3);  // Shallow depth = 3
    
    // Step 12: Compare deep before shallow
    bool deepBefore = batchCompare(deepWeak, shallowWeak);
    
    // Step 13: Create null NodeWeak
    reaction::NodeWeak nullWeak;
    
    // Step 14: Compare null with shallow
    bool nullFirst = batchCompare(nullWeak, shallowWeak);
    
    // Step 15: Return depth ordering snapshot
    return DepthOrderingSnapshot{
        .initialComparatorResult = initialResult,
        .shallowBeforeDeep = shallowBefore,
        .deepBeforeShallow = deepBefore,
        .nullComesFirst = nullFirst
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>
